explanations.1: This special first line tells Docker to use an advanced Dockerfile syntax version that unlocks extra features like cache mounts, which help speed up image rebuilds.
explanations.16: This pulls from a custom base image called 'blog-docusaurus' that you've already built elsewhere; it comes pre-loaded with Node.js and Docusaurus so we don't have to install them here.
explanations.19: We're re-declaring the APP_HOME variable here because Docker arguments don't automatically carry through after a FROM statement, so we need to repeat it.
explanations.25: The --mount options here are caching layers: they tell Docker to reuse downloaded packages across builds instead of re-downloading them every time, making rebuilds much faster.
explanations.38: This git command prevents errors when Docker tries to access the app folder; it tells git 'it's okay, this folder is safe even though you didn't clone it yourself'.
explanations.48: This creates a special file that lets the 'node' user run any command with 'sudo' without needing to type a password, which is convenient in a development container.
explanations.79: The --mount here is similar to line 25, but with uid and gid parameters that ensure the yarn cache folder has the correct ownership so the non-root user can access it.
